title: Hair and Nail Care - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Hair and Nail Care 13 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Veterans CITYTEAM PORTLAND NIGHT STRIKE NIGHT STRIKE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides access to grooming and personal hygiene supplies; haircuts and foot washing; a hot meal; and an opportunity to have clothing and sleeping bags replaced. All resources are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Also provides volunteer opportunities. 214 W Burnside Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: People experiencing homelessness Hours: Thursdays 7:30pm-9:30pm aside from 1st and 2nd Thursdays in June; 4th Thursday in November; 2nd Thursday in December; 1st Thursday in January Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INIATIVES AT MIDLAND LIBRARY MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 805 SE 122nd Avenue Portland, OR 97233 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Thursday 11am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details CULTIVATE INITIATIVES CULTIVATE INITIATIVES AT LENTS WELLNESS FAIR MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a mobile van for showers, clothing, haircuts, hygiene products, health education, and provider referrals. Some services are conducted by nursing students and depend on volunteers. 4244 SE 91st Avenue Portland, OR 97266 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday 11am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV DAY CENTER HIV SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center serving hot breakfast and lunches, access to phones and computers, and recreational activities such as art therapy, games, and video-based media. Also provides clothing, showers, mail services, and hygiene supplies. Guests have access to laundry services, massages, acupuncture, foot care, and haircuts on a sign-up basis. 2941 NE Ainsworth Street Basement Portland, OR 97211 Eligibility: Individuals who are HIV-positive and have incomes no greater than 300% of the federal poverty level Hours: Breakfast: Monday-Friday 9am-10:30am Lunch: Monday-Friday 1pm All other services: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (503) 460-3822 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ELEVATE HIM PROFESSIONAL CLOTHING AND HAIRCUTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides interview and work-ready clothing to help with the transition into the workforce and for appointments that require a professional appearance. Also provides professional haircuts. Clothing donations accepted. 1513 SW Marlow Avenue Suite A Portland, OR 97225 Eligibility: Males age 12 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm by appointment Email (503) 810-1607 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS ACCESS CENTER STREET LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides short-term overnight shelter, two meals per day, and cleanup facilities for 30 homeless and street dependent youth attempting to make changes in their lives. Youth may access clothing, medical assistance, and employment education support. When severe weather is declared by the city, 5 additional beds will open. 1635 SW Alder Street Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Displaced youth ages 16-24. Youth must be working with a case manager on a plan in order to access a bed on a continuing basis. Hours: 7 days per week 9pm-9am Email (503) 432-3986 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER HOMELESS DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A day center that provides meals, a place to rest and socialize, housing resource materials, and WiFi. On-site housing liaison is available during the week. Mental health services are sometimes available on-site through partner agency. 12264 SW Hall Boulevard Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Day center hours: 7 days per week 7am-7pm -Breakfast 9am-10:15am -Lunch noon-1pm -Social Services 1pm-2pm -Showers by appointment only Email (503) 624-4666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects people with local churches and service agencies. Basic goods and services are offered to clients as they are donated. Services available may include, but are not limited to: bicycles, breakfast/lunch bags, clothing closets (adults and children), community meals, counseling services, diapers, haircuts, H.U.G.S. (hats, umbrellas, gloves, scarves, and socks), kitchen supplies, laundry service, bedding/linens, personal grooming supplies/toiletries, over-the-counter medications, personal care kits, physical therapy, showers, water bill assistance, and firewood. Accepts donations of bedding and linen, clothing, and personal grooming supplies. 200 S College Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Thursday 10am-1pm Email (503) 537-3999 Get Directions Visit Website More Details P:EAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in center that offers arts and music, mentoring, educational, and outdoor recreational programs. Additional supportive services include bus tickets, haircuts, clothing, and support for pregnancy and parenting. Also offers meals and cooking classes. Accepts donations of food, clothing, and camping gear. 338 NW 6th Avenue Portland, OR 97205 Eligibility: Ages 15-24 experiencing homelessness Hours: Tuesday-Friday: 9am-2pm Breakfast at 9am and lunch at noon to those served by p:ear Donations: Monday-Friday 8am-3pm Email (503) 228-6677 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PDX SAINTS LOVE DAY CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a day center for sheltering, hot meals, haircuts, clothing, public restrooms, bus passes, wound care, hygiene kits, OHP and SNAP form assistance, ID and birth record replacement and payment assistance, resources for housing navigation and shelter referrals, detox referrals, overdose prevention, and Narcan supplies. Community Health Workers (CHWs), Peer Wellness Specialists (PWS), and Peer Support Specialists (PSS) are available to assist with resources, connections to health care, and case management. 247 SE 82nd Avenue Portland, OR 97216 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Day Center: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm, Thanksgiving and Christmas 9am-noon Hot meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Haircuts: Monday 10am-2pm Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details THE FATHER'S HEART Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop-in day center with services that include two meals a day with a sack lunch to-go, showers by appointment (not wheelchair accessible), haircuts, computer and telephone access, blankets, clothing, shoes, and hygiene items. Referrals to resources surrounding housing, mental health, and substance abuse. Assistance with the determination of homelessness letters to obtain an Oregon ID. 603 12th Street Oregon City, OR 97045 Eligibility: Homeless adults age 18 and older and adults with families Hours: Day Center and meals: Monday-Friday 9am-2pm Showers: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Email (503) 722-9780 Get Directions Visit Website More Details TRANSITION PROJECTS RESOURCE CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance in accessing services and agencies to help people transition out of homelessness into stable housing. The center offers showers, laundry, haircuts, clothing, lockers, and mailing address service. 650 NW Irving Street Portland, OR 97209 Eligibility: Age 18 and older experiencing homelessness Hours: Resource Center: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays: 8:30am-3pm. All service intakes end 15 minutes before close. 1st Thursday and 3rd Tuesday of the month the Resource Center closes at noon. Restroom and showers: Monday-Friday 8:30am-7pm, Saturday/Sunday/all holidays: 8:30am-3pm Clothing: Monday/Friday 10am-11am, Wednesday 1pm-2pm Haircuts: Wednesday-Friday, times vary Mailroom: Monday-Friday 8:30am-10am and 12:45pm-3:30pm, Saturday 1pm-3pm (503) 280-4700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VETERANS STAND DOWNS AND VETERANS EVENTS STAND DOWN EVENTS VETERANS STAND DOWN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an annual health and wellness resource event. Services may include health screening, medical and dental care, vision, housing resources, counseling and substance abuse referrals, haircuts, pet care, job fairs, help accessing VA services and benefits, DMV and legal assistance. Meals, clothing, and hygiene supplies may also be offered. Eligibility: Veterans and military personnel; focus is generally on homeless and at-risk veterans Hours: Varies by event Visit Website More Details CITYTEAM NIGHT STRIKE CULTIVATE INITIATIVES MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE CULTIVATE INITIATIVES MOBILE SHOWERS AND HYGIENE ECUMENICAL MINISTRIES OF OREGON HIV SERVICES ELEVATE HIM PROFESSIONAL CLOTHING AND HAIRCUTS JANUS YOUTH PROGRAMS STREET LIGHT YOUTH SHELTER JUST COMPASSION RESOURCE CENTER HOMELESS DAY CENTER LOVE INC NEWBERG GAP MINISTRIES P:EAR P:EAR PDX SAINTS LOVE DAY CENTER THE FATHER'S HEART THE FATHER'S HEART TRANSITION PROJECTS RESOURCE CENTER
